svg.title: Deployment Diagram - Delivery Platform System (v3.0 - Perfect)

g.text: Deployment Diagram - Delivery Platform System (v3.0 - Perfect)

g.data-entity: Cloud Provider .AWS . DigitalOcean..n.Region. Pakistan per NFR-21.
g.id: cluster_Cloud Provider .AWS . DigitalOcean..n.Region. Pakistan per NFR-21.

g.text: Each service runs as a Deployment with ≥2 replicas
Horizontal Pod Autoscaling based on CPU/memory.
Internal service discovery via Kubernetes DNS.
Circuit breakers (Resilience4j) on all inter-service REST calls.
Each service owns its own database schema
(or dedicated database) to ensure loose coupling.
Read replicas used for product queries (NFR-06).
Daily backups, RPO ≤ 1 hour, RTO ≤ 4 hours (NFR-09).
All personal data stored in Pakistan per NFR-21.
Stores ephemeral live location data (retention 24h, SEC-21)
and user session tokens for API Gateway.
Kafka or RabbitMQ for asynchronous event-driven communication.
Ensures loose coupling and eventual consistency (Saga pattern).
Services use the Outbox pattern to reliably publish events.
AWS S3 or DigitalOcean Spaces (region: Pakistan).
Stores images, bill pictures, ID cards, legal agreements.
Stores all client actions, security events, and financial
Write-only for ClientSvc; read access restricted.
ClientSvc acts as the audit logging facade.
which then writes immutable records to AuditLogDB.
